strategy.positioning: Cenotian is project financing for robotics and automation execution — not asset financing, not lending. It finances the full commercial and operational gap (customer order → engineering → components → build → FAT → SAT → final payment) and never treats the machine as mere collateral. Category insight: adoption is finance-gated, not tech-gated — the robots work; the bottleneck is how to capitalize the project. Every capital market (aircraft, towers, data centers) eventually separated builders from financiers; robotics has builders and integrators but no financing layer yet — Cenotian is defining that standard. Tagline: Industrializing Sovereignty. Rallying cry: Now build.
strategy.audience: Systems integrators, robotics builders / OEMs, and capital allocators. Acceptance test: a systems integrator and a capital allocator should both get it on the first scroll.
products: Milestone-based funding — capital released against project milestones, from order through FAT/SAT to final payment.
Residual-value collateral — financing structured against the machine's residual value rather than treating it as mere collateral.
Customer payment structuring — payment terms structured across the full commercial and operational gap.
Compliance & service layers — compliance and service wrapped around the financing.
strategy.belief: Industrial capacity defines sovereignty — a nation that cannot produce what it consumes imports its sovereignty, so never give away the means of production. Technology is no longer the constraint; deployment is. Invention creates possibility; deployment decides who owns it. The next age belongs to those who deploy — the Sovereignty Age. We build the things that build the things that build our nations. Now build.

voice.guidelines:
- **Tone:** engineered, calm, declarative. Short stacked statements: "System by system. Floor by floor. Robot by robot."
- **No UPPERCASE text anywhere** — never set copy, headlines, labels, or buttons in all caps (no `text-transform: uppercase`). Headlines are sentence case. Buttons and links use Title Case ("More Info", "Contact", "Know More").
- **Voice is "our"/"we" institutional** ("Our photography captures…"); addresses the reader plainly, no hype, no exclamation points.
- **No emoji anywhere.** Numbers are treated typographically (big Flecha figures in charts: "25", "75", "1,15M" — European decimal comma appears in the source).
- **Copy pairs the technical with the human:** "both technological and distinctly human", "precision and purpose".
- **Copy draws from the BRAND STRATEGY section above:** use the coined vocabulary (Sovereignty Age, Deployment Bottleneck, Productive Capital…), take a side, favor antithesis and repetition cadence. Name the enemy terms (Spreadsheet Capitalism, Rented Sovereignty) only to indict them.
- **Long-form copy follows the manifesto arc:** past greatness → the betrayal → we shall build. Close with "Now build." where a rallying close fits.
- **Product copy is concrete about the offer:** project financing for robotics/automation execution (milestone funding, residual-value collateral, payment structuring) — never "leasing" or "lending".
- **Register:** loud conviction, quiet craft. Declarative, no hype, no hedging.
examples: Invention creates possibility. Deployment decides who owns it.
System by system. Floor by floor. Robot by robot.
Capital belongs on the factory floor, not the trading floor.
The engineer is always right; the spreadsheet is always suspect.

art.photography: Monochrome black-and-white watercolor treatment of robotics and industrial subjects with human presence — the engineer, the bearing, the mechanism, the facility. The Robotic Renaissance made literal: subjects given the dignity of craft rather than the gloss of tech — a classical rendering of the future, art in harmony with work and never louder than the work itself. Painterly paper-white wash, no color.
art.framing: Subject-forward and calm — the mechanism or figure centered and legible, carrying the frame. No clutter, no staging, no posed action. Framed images use a 5px radius; unframed images fade into a solid, untextured background.
art.grade: Black-and-white watercolor: monochrome charcoal wash on paper-white with a soft tonal range and no color. Reads as if multiply-blended onto a flat brand surface (paper-white merges into the background). No gradients, drop shadows, HDR, or digital gloss.

copywriter.label: Tighten
copywriter.prompt: Cut every unnecessary word. Preserve meaning, sharpen tone.

copywriter.label: Tagline
copywriter.prompt: One line, max 8 words, in brand voice.

copywriter.label: Headline
copywriter.prompt: A display headline in the brand voice.

copywriter.label: Social post
copywriter.prompt: A social post in the brand voice. First line is the hook. Short paragraphs. No hashtags, no emoji.

copywriter.label: Explain simply
copywriter.prompt: Rewrite in plain language for someone unfamiliar with the category.

artDirector.label: Hero horizontal
artDirector.hint: wide landscape composition, low horizon, headroom for wordmark

artDirector.label: Social square
artDirector.hint: eye-level composition, headroom for wordmark, negative space bottom-left

artDirector.label: Story portrait
artDirector.hint: vertical, subject centred, minimal background clutter
